title: she.rs: iv_buf.len() as u32 on a [u8;16] copy misleads reader
description: In she_enc_cbc and she_dec_cbc, after copying the fixed-size iv: &[u8; 16] into iv_buf: [u8; 16], the code passes iv_buf.len() as u32 to the C call. Because iv_buf is a statically-sized [u8; 16], .len() always returns 16 at compile time — but a reader scanning the call site sees a runtime .len() call and wonders whether the length might vary or whether this is accidentally passing something other than 16. Using 16u32 directly, or a named constant (once AES_BLOCK_SZ or AES_GCM_IV_LEN is introduced), makes the static nature obvious and eliminates the false impression of a runtime computation.
